perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a perpetual, non- exclusive, royalty-free license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Copyrights for use in the Company Field throughout the world.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a temporary, non- exclusive, royalty-free license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Copyrights for use in the Company Field throughout the world.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'perpetual' to 'temporary' introduces a contradiction with Section 12.1(a)(iv), which states that the license for Copyrights, Know-How, and Patents is 'in perpetuity.' The modification makes it unclear whether the copyright license is intended to last forever or for a limited time, creating uncertainty about the rights of the Company to use the Arizona Licensed Copyrights. This change contradicts the overall intention for the duration of copyrights, know-how and patents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated pursuant to the provisions hereof, the term of this Agreement and the licenses and other grants of rights (and related obligations) under this Agreement shall (i) with respect to the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, be for the Arizona Trademark License Term, (ii) with respect to the Diamond Licensed Trademarks, be for the Diamond Trademark License Term, (iii) with respect to the Phase- Out Marks, be for the term set forth in Section 6.6, and (iv) with respect to Copyrights, Know-How and Patents, be in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'perpetual' to 'temporary' in the license grant directly contradicts section 12.1(a)(iv), which explicitly states that the license for Copyrights, Know-How and Patents is 'in perpetuity.' This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the duration of the copyright license, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as it relates to the Arizona Licensed Copyrights. This satisfies the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a limited, non- exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a unlimited, non- exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'limited' to 'unlimited' concerning the license for Arizona Licensed Trademarks creates an in-text contradiction with the explicit limitation of the license to the 'Arizona Trademark License Term.' The original text specifies that the license is only for a specific duration. Making the license 'unlimited' conflicts with the fixed term described in Section 1.1(n) and Section 12.1, making the scope and duration of the trademark license ambiguous, in turn, it also contradicts the Section 6.1, since the Arizona trademark licensed trademark is for the Arizona Trademark License Term, and the change in the text from limited to unlimited, create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a limited, non- exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'limited' to 'unlimited' in the license grant directly contradicts the Arizona Trademark License Term, as the original text explicitly limits the license to this term. The contradicted text, Section 6.1, grants a license specifically 'for the Arizona Trademark License Term.' By removing the 'limited' nature of the license, it now implies a potentially perpetual license, which clashes with the stated duration in Section 6.1.

perturbation.original_text: Except as expressly provided in the Stock Purchase Agreement or herein, each Party reserves its and its Affiliates' rights in and to all Intellectual Property (including with respect to the use, registration and licensing thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: Except as expressly provided in the Stock Purchase Agreement or herein, each Party gives up its and its Affiliates' rights in and to all Intellectual Property (including with respect to the use, registration and licensing thereof).
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'reserves' with 'gives up' significantly alters the fundamental premise of the agreement. The original text reinforces that each party retains ownership and rights to their IP, except for the specific licenses granted. The modified text states that parties relinquish all IP rights which contradicts the purpose of the licenses, specifically with Section 8.1 and 8.2, which clearly states who owns what, and it would also make the rest of the IP agreement absurd to exist. This change would lead to considerable confusion and legal disputes about intellectual property ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Ownership of Arizona Licensed IP. The Buyer Entities acknowledge and agree that (a) Arizona and its Affiliates own the Arizona Licensed IP (other than the Arizona Licensed Trademarks), (b) AWI Licensing LLC owns the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, (b) neither the Company, nor its Affiliates or its Sublicensees, will acquire any ownership rights in the Arizona Licensed IP, and (c) the Company shall not represent or make any claim that it has an ownership interest in any Arizona Licensed IP. Without limitation to the foregoing, the Company shall not file applications to register any Arizona Licensed IP or assist any person in doing the same, or contest, challenge, or otherwise take any action adverse to Arizona's and its Affiliates' ownership of or rights in and to the Arizona Licensed IP, or assist any person in doing the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'reserves' with 'gives up', indicating that each party relinquishes all rights to their Intellectual Property. This directly contradicts section 8.1, which states that Arizona and its affiliates own the Arizona Licensed IP and that the Company shall not acquire any ownership rights. The original text reinforces each party retaining their IP rights, whereas the changed text gives them up, thus creating a strong contradiction as the agreement states clearly the opposite of the changed text.
